item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Looks solid to me! If Dr Rignaros declines the edit though, I will defer to him as co-author.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Damn, this is a real sad one. Honestly the plot here is pretty tightly knit, I feel most links will just come across as shoehorned. However, the mention of Dr. Wondertainment can be turned into a crosslink to the GoI's hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Subsequent Foundation investigation of Mr. Sharp's home resulted in the discovery of a workshop located in the basement, outfitted with modified blacklights. Exposure of Mr. Sharp's previously-recovered notebook to the blacklights revealed a series of diagrams believed to be designs for preliminary prototypes of SCP-2356. Additionally, various notes on separate pages confirmed Huey Sharp's affiliation with the Doctor Wondertainment product line and his intention of adapting SCP-2356 into a Wondertainment toy targeting children currently in hospital treatment.

To this:

Subsequent Foundation investigation of Mr. Sharp's home resulted in the discovery of a workshop located in the basement, outfitted with modified blacklights. Exposure of Mr. Sharp's previously-recovered notebook to the blacklights revealed a series of diagrams believed to be designs for preliminary prototypes of SCP-2356. Additionally, various notes on separate pages confirmed Huey Sharp's affiliation with the Doctor Wondertainment product line and his intention of adapting SCP-2356 into a Wondertainment toy targeting children currently in hospital treatment.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait a quick minute.

The foundation recovered the item alongside some toys that seemed to have come from nowhere. And the article mentions how the object can be triggered by extremely depressed people without physical contact. And the son of whoever created this thing died shortly before recovery.

Wow, that is some excellent background storytelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still find it not believable about the recovery. It was just a thing left behind and unclaimed. That alone is no grounds for the Foundation to care about the thing, unless they see it do something anomalous. Also, I doubt if the Wondertainment reference is truly needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang it.
Saltwater isn't good for keyboards, people.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unexpectedly sweet, I like that, not every scip has to be horrific.

Seems a little basic though, a dad creates a magic wand to cheer up his son with leukemia, and nothing more to it. The Wondertainment connection explains how he created something that's literally magic, and the Foundation staking out the son's grave to try and capture the father adds a little edge. But overall it's a little bland for me.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unexpectedly moving. Very nice indeed. Just enough to stimulate your imagination, not enough to completely satisfy your curiosity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to everyone in chat who took a look at this, and the helpful posters in the forums! You guys are the best.

Writing this piece has been quite the process! The beginnings of the SCP concept in development can be read through here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1747782/a-new-wondertainment-product

Use the Ideas and Brainstorming forum, kids! That's where magic happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to thank my co-author Zyn for helping me create this SCP. She's been a great help in editing and ideas for this.

Thanks to ThatGuyRiverside, Aiden Eldritch, and MrWrong for helping out in the forums.
Thanks to MacLeod, overandover, JackIke, Jekeled, Conwell, Athenodora, thekillerax, GibberingEloquence, SpectralDragon, and Tuomey for reading and commenting in chat.

This SCP is pretty sad but does have a good moral in it too. We want you to consider the importance of how the bow tie shows the child's relationship with his father.
